topic: calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification

scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: This research topic is definitely feasible on your MacBook M4 with 36GB RAM and MPS GPU acceleration, as calibration-aware active learning approaches are computationally manageable for medical imaging tasks. I recommend using the CheXpert chest X-ray dataset (over 200K images), the Diabetic Retinopathy Detection dataset (over 35K fundus images), or the BraTS brain tumor MRI dataset (over 2,000 cases) as they're well-suited for this research. parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper investigates the phenomenon of 'grokking' in neural networks through systematic experiments across various algorithmic tasks, introducing new metrics like 'grokking speed' and 'extrapolation gap' to quantify and analyze this generalization behavior.
method_or_focus: The systematic investigation of grokking across different conditions (regularization, noise, extrapolation) and the introduction of metrics to quantify grokking behavior.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: Based on full paper reading: The systematic investigation of grokking across different conditions (regularization, noise, extrapolation) and the introduction of metrics to quantify grokking behavior.

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
parsed_papers.core_claim: Extends automated scientific discovery with stronger search and review-like iteration, motivating stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
limitations: The system's output is currently at a 'workshop-level' of quality, not yet at a top-tier conference standard.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper does not specify which scientific domains the system is applied to, leaving a gap for domain-specific validation and adaptation.
